roadmap.title: Tidy the planning folder and lay down the new structure
Build the foundation layer the rest of the system sits on
Build the house-style layer above the foundation
Break the driver and authoring files into smaller pieces
Remove any leftover version stamps and stop new ones at commit time
Build the part that reads books and extracts the structured material
Build the part that remembers things across books
Build the part that brings in carefully-sourced outside knowledge
Wire the three new pieces into the pipeline in the right order
Add three more book-kinds the factory knows how to handle
Build the multi-layer capstone — the closing chapter writer
Switch on the outside-knowledge gatherer for forward books
Pilot the visual-deck capability before the wider rollout
Add the visual-deck classifier and the human-review markers
Plan the rollout to the larger books with cost guardrails
Set up the design tokens and the dashboard app shell
Build the front page that ties the three views together
Build the architecture and infrastructure visualizations
Build the live execution dashboard with streaming updates
Bring every shipped book onto the unified metadata schema
Ship the Kitab al-Riyad addendum episode
Ship the Master and the Disciple preface and addendum
Extend the ship checklist with the new readiness gates

waves.name: Foundation
waves.plain: Tidy up, then build the layers everything sits on. Smaller files, cleaner imports, version stamps gone.

waves.name: Intelligence
waves.plain: Three new pieces that read, remember, and enrich — the brain that makes the factory smarter over time.

waves.name: More book kinds
waves.plain: Teach the factory to handle three additional kinds of books and the multi-layer closing-chapter style.

waves.name: Dashboard
waves.plain: Replace the static plan files with this living dashboard. You're looking at it.

waves.name: Backfill
waves.plain: Bring the already-shipped books onto the new schema and add the extra ship-readiness checks.

debt.title: The publish dry-run accidentally modifies source files
debt.plain: When you ask the publish step to do a dry run, it claims to write nothing but actually changes a few episode files. Workaround is to undo with git after each dry-run. Real fix not yet done.

debt.title: Resume after a crashed run is stuck thinking it's still running
debt.plain: If the factory crashes mid-step, the state file still says 'running' and the next resume refuses to start. Workaround is to retry the phase explicitly. Real fix needs a heartbeat check.
